'''Bursk''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="CVD" /> who served as a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a conflict between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="CCG" /> He<ref name="CSWE" /> attained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Sergeant major/Legends|sergeant major]]<ref name="CCG" /> by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> the Empire discovered [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]], the Alliance's [[Alliance High Command/Legends|headquarters]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. The discovery led to the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], which included a [[Ground warfare/Legends|ground assault]] on the base itself.<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref>

Bursk, a cunning planner, took part in the assault as part of the Imperial [[Blizzard Force/Legends|Blizzard Force]], and was the leader of one of the most brutal [[Cold assault stormtrooper|snowtrooper]] [[Platoon/Legends|platoons]], coordinating attacks and managing troop movements.<ref name="CCG" /> Ultimately, the Battle of Hoth resulted in an Imperial victory, with the Alliance forced to evacuate the planet.<ref name="ESB" /> At some point around that [[Time/Legends|time]], Bursk stood behind another snowtrooper, [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Narthax]].<ref name="AFA">''[[Star Wars: The Action Figure Archive]]''</ref> Bursk wore full [[Snowtrooper armor/Legends|snowtrooper armor]], and was armed with an [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifle]].<ref name="CCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Wampa_pen_top.jpg|right|thumb||250px|Bursk (rightmost in photo) was one of the snowtroopers filmed for the ''Empire Strikes Back'' deleted scene outside the wampa pen.]]
Bursk first appeared in a Battle of Hoth deleted scene for the [[1980]] film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], during which he was part of a group of snowtroopers who were tricked into approaching a [[Wampa/Legends|wampa]] pen after the [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] tore off the door's warning label, provoking the wampas within.<ref name="Deleted scene">{{HomeVideoCite|set=Star Wars: The Complete Saga|scene=Wampa Scenes|nolink=1}}</ref> During production, the scenes where [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and the snowtroopers initially entered Echo Base were filmed in [[Wikipedia:England|England's]] [[Elstree Studios]] between [[March]] and [[April]] [[1979]].<ref name="The Making of The Empire Strikes Back">''[[The Making of The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref>

That deleted scene was later used as the basis for the short story "[[One Step Ahead]]," published in the [[1996]] [[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|second edition]] of ''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]''.<ref name="GG3">{{WEGCite|story=One Step Ahead|book=Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)}}</ref> In spring 1996,<ref name="Date">The "[[Lucasfilm's Latest]]" and "[[Scoring the Trilogy]]" departments of [[Star Wars Insider 29|''Star Wars Insider'' 29]] collectively indicate that this issue was published between the release of the [[Shadows of the Empire (soundtrack)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' soundtrack]] (which {{Amazon|url=Star-Wars-Shadows-The-Empire/dp/B0000014YD|text=Joel McNeely: Star Wars: Shadows Of The Empire: Music}} dates to [[April 23]], [[1996]]) and the release of the book ''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'' (which {{Amazon|url=Secrets-Star-Wars-Shadows-Empire/dp/0345402367|text=The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire}} dates to [[April 30]], 1996). Given that ''Star Wars Insider'' was published quarterly during this time, this indicates a Spring 1996 release for issue 29.</ref> that deleted scene was included, along with photos, in the "[[Prequel Update]]" article for [[Star Wars Insider 29|''Star Wars Insider'' 29]].<ref name="Prequel Update">{{InsiderCite|29|Prequel Update}}</ref> In fall of [[1998]], the CD-ROM ''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' also included the deleted scene and photos of the snowtroopers. Although ''Behind the Magic'' describes the wampas attacking all the snowtroopers in the scene,<ref name="BTM">''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''</ref> the filmed scene only shows one of the snowtroopers entering the pen and being attacked by the wampas.<ref name="Deleted scene" />

In [[November]] of 1998, Bursk was first identified by name in a card for the ''[[Special Edition Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]].<ref name="CCG" /> In [[2011]], the deleted scene with the snowtroopers was officially released as part of the ''[[Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' [[Wikipedia:Blu-ray Disc|Blu-ray]] set.<ref name="Deleted scene" /> Although "One Step Ahead" [[canon]]izes the event of wampas attacking snowtroopers in a story based off the deleted scene,<ref name="GG3" /> no source makes an in-universe link between production photos of Bursk and the short story. Therefore, this article avoids linking Bursk to any of the snowtroopers in "One Step Ahead."
